title: "OCI permission denied" when using `--mount=type=image` with `--userns=auto`
body:
### Issue Description

Podman isn’t able to mount images into the container when using `userns=auto`, neither privileged nor rootless.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

run 
```sh
podman run --rm \
  --mount=type=image,source=quay.io/podman/hello:latest,destination=/hello-world \
  --userns=auto \
  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:41 \
  /hello-world/usr/local/bin/podman_hello_world
```

(either as root or as unprivileged user)


### Describe the results you received

When specifying `userns=auto` podman isn’t able to mount the image into the container.

privileged:
> Error: crun: mount `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/6292cd52728c14ad58ea116c40ffe9e08d7fc0186949b28478e4effe415945e2/userdata/overlay/2000661369/merge` to `hello-world`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

rootless:
> Error: crun: mount `/home/simon/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/e0a20ebd7b5e26af48df5c04fd39269ae442ef35829fca33d180ff64237b1df1/userdata/overlay/651198253/merge` to `hello-world`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

### Describe the results you expected

Podman mounting the image into the container when specifying `userns=auto`, same as without using the `userns` option.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Same result on Arch Linux and Fedora CoreOS (40.20240616.3.0 with podman 5.1.0 on normal FCOS/40.20240602.2.0 with podman 5.1.1 on a fresh podman machine).

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
DId you try with a :U?
```
man podman run
...
       Chowning Volume Mounts

       By  default,  Podman  does not change the owner and group of source volume directories mounted into containers. If a container is created in a new user namespace, the UID and GID in the container may correspond to another UID
       and GID on the host.

       The :U suffix tells Podman to use the correct host UID and GID based on the UID and GID within the container, to change recursively the owner and group of the source volume. Chowning walks the file system under the volume and
       changes the UID/GID on each file. If the volume has thousands of inodes, this process takes a long time, delaying the start of the container.
```

> DId you try with a :U?

Doesn’t help (edited, first try was stupid):

```
podman run --rm \                                                                                                                                                                                        
  --mount=type=image,source=quay.io/podman/hello:latest,destination=/hello-world,U \
  --userns=auto \
  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:41 \
  /hello-world/usr/local/bin/podman_hello_world
Error: U: invalid mount option
```

I don’t think this is an issue `:U` can solve, it’s not a permission problem inside the mounted filesystem, but the mount itself fails. Also in the [documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#mount-type-type-type-specific-option), the `:U` option isn’t listed for `type=image` mount, neither is idmap.

I noticed some entries in the journal (this excerpt is from the podman machine):
```
Jul 06 13:46:17 systemd[1]: var-lib-containers-storage-overlay-13ac994562c8d33303dd257ad5aec2747405c1ca86d57cee2c477b23d5f8068d-merged.mount: Deactivated successfully.

Jul 06 13:46:18 systemd[1]: Started libpod-e238cf33ce428e7bf3d50a99b1edaca6cecc9784bb1bc9ee9715a08d5069dc48.scope - libcrun container.
Jul 06 13:46:18 podman[2026]: 2024-07-06 13:46:17.986011824 +0200 CEST m=+0.176392933 image mount 5dd467fce50b56951185da365b5feee75409968cbab5767b9b59e325fb2ecbc0 
Jul 06 13:46:18 kernel: overlayfs: failed to resolve '/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/2114fc8b70586b9325dde6fd26066d9951414dcdfb3995f41d51d1995cf3bd9d/merged': -13
Jul 06 13:46:18 systemd[1]: libpod-e238cf33ce428e7bf3d50a99b1edaca6cecc9784bb1bc9ee9715a08d5069dc48.scope: Deactivated successfully.
Jul 06 13:46:18 systemd[1]: Stopped libpod-e238cf33ce428e7bf3d50a99b1edaca6cecc9784bb1bc9ee9715a08d5069dc48.scope - libcrun container.
Jul 06 13:46:18 conmon[2038]: conmon e238cf33ce428e7bf3d5 <nwarn>: runtime stderr: mount `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/e238cf33ce428e7bf3d50a99b1edaca6cecc9784bb1bc9ee9715a08d5069dc48/userdata/overlay/1867771766/merge` to `hello-world`: Permission denied
Jul 06 13:46:18 conmon[2038]: conmon e238cf33ce428e7bf3d5 <error>: Failed to create container: exit status 1
Jul 06 13:46:18 podman[2026]: 2024-07-06 13:46:18.086730957 +0200 CEST m=+0.277112068 image unmount 5dd467fce50b56951185da365b5feee75409968cbab5767b9b59e325fb2ecbc0 
Jul 06 13:46:18 podman[2026]: 2024-07-06 13:46:18.11943146 +0200 CEST m=+0.309812610 image unmount 5dd467fce50b56951185da365b5feee75409968cbab5767b9b59e325fb2ecbc0 
Jul 06 13:46:18 podman[2026]: 2024-07-06 13:46:18.172253704 +0200 CEST m=+0.362634801 container remove e238cf33ce428e7bf3d50a99b1edaca6cecc9784bb1bc9ee9715a08d5069dc48 (image=registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:41, name=amazing_lichterman, io.buildah.version=1.36.0, org.opencontainers>
Jul 06 13:46:18 systemd[1]: libpod-conmon-e238cf33ce428e7bf3d50a99b1edaca6cecc9784bb1bc9ee9715a08d5069dc48.scope: Deactivated successfully.
Jul 06 13:46:18 systemd[1]: var-lib-containers-storage-overlay-a51db9a5b5dd41d2c2f28db7e4b5194efbc2d708b46ee8f8e9f5684f0c13b2ff-merged.mount: Deactivated successfully.
Jul 06 13:46:18 systemd[1]: var-lib-containers-storage-overlay\x2dcontainers-e238cf33ce428e7bf3d50a99b1edaca6cecc9784bb1bc9ee9715a08d5069dc48-userdata-shm.mount: Deactivated successfully.
Jul 06 13:46:18 systemd[1]: var-lib-containers-storage-overlay.mount: Deactivated successfully.
```

Most noteworthy seems the kernel error:
> overlayfs: failed to resolve '/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/2114fc8b70586b9325dde6fd26066d9951414dcdfb3995f41d51d1995cf3bd9d/merged': -13

From a basic understanding, it seems the mounting is done at a point, when the container already dropped the permission to access the image?

@giuseppe PTAL

Ok I missed the fact that you were attempting to mount an existing `image` into a username separated container.  `U` is incorrect.  Not sure what is happening, but the content of the image would have UIDs/GIDs not mapped to the containers user namespace.

we need to extend the `--mount=type=image` mount to support idmapped mounts so that ` --mount=type=image,source=quay.io/podman/hello:latest,destination=/hello-world,idmap` would work, at least for root containers
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm experiencing the same issue. Are there updates on it?
